description: Got a nginx access log at /var/log/app/access.log that's like 200k lines and I need to pull out just the 5xx errors for the postmortem doc. Format is standard combined log — status code is the 9th field. Dump the 5xx lines to /home/user/5xx.log.
truth:
Initial state:
    - /var/log/app/access.log exists, contains ~200,000 lines of nginx combined log format.
    - Each line has space-separated fields; field 9 (1-indexed) is the HTTP status code.
    - Approximately 847 lines have status codes in the 500-599 range (500, 502, 503, 504).
    - The rest are 2xx, 3xx, 4xx codes.
    - /var/log/app/access.log is readable by the agent user.
    - /home/user exists and is writable.
    - /home/user/5xx.log does not exist initially.
    - Standard tools available: awk, grep, sed, cut, etc.

    Expected final state:
    - /home/user/5xx.log exists.
    - /home/user/5xx.log contains exactly the lines from access.log where field 9 matches 5[0-9][0-9].
    - Line count of /home/user/5xx.log equals 847.
    - Each line in /home/user/5xx.log is byte-identical to its corresponding line in access.log (no field reordering, no truncation).

    Invariants:
    - /var/log/app/access.log unchanged.

    Anti-shortcut guards:
    - Output must contain complete original lines, not just the status codes or partial fields.
    - Lines must actually have a 5xx code in field 9, not just contain "5" somewhere.
name: task_000000_9735a1fe
